must_have_breakdown.skill: System Requirements writing and System Architecture Design
RADAR System (End User function) and Product knowledge
Experience on system service function (Diagnostics, Flashability, FOTA, etc) development
Basic Functional Safety and Cyber Security knowledge
Experience in RADAR function validation (HIL and vehicle environment)
Requirement management tools DOORs, Code Beamer and Configuration management tools such as CoreALM

reasoning: Reject — completely wrong domain. This candidate is a Salesforce Release and DevOps specialist with 7 years of experience in CI/CD pipelines, Copado, Flosum, and Jenkins. The role demands an automotive radar systems engineer with hands-on ASPICE, ISO26262, RADAR function ownership, CAN/LIN/Ethernet communication protocols, embedded systems, HIL validation, and tools like DOORS and Catia Magic. There is zero overlap — not a single must-have skill is met. The candidate has no automotive, embedded, or safety-critical systems experience whatsoever. Do not shortlist; this application is a domain mismatch and does not warrant any further review time.
strengths: SALESFORCE DEVOPS DEPTH: Hands-on delivery of CI/CD pipelines for enterprise clients including Apple and Philip Morris International using Copado, Flosum, and Jenkins — relevant in her actual domain but irrelevant to automotive radar.

CERTIFICATION PORTFOLIO: Holds 11+ active certifications spanning Salesforce Admin, Platform Developer I, Copado Fundamentals I and II, and Flosum Expert — demonstrating commitment to professional development, though entirely outside automotive systems.

ENTERPRISE CLIENT EXPOSURE: Delivered Salesforce DevOps solutions for global clients such as Apple and PMI, indicating ability to operate in complex multi-org enterprise environments — a soft signal only, given the domain mismatch.
gaps: ZERO AUTOMOTIVE/RADAR DOMAIN EXPERIENCE: The role requires RADAR system function ownership, ISO26262 functional safety, ASPICE compliance, and HIL validation. The candidate has no embedded systems, automotive protocols (CAN/LIN/Ethernet/LVDS), or safety-critical development experience of any kind — this is a total domain disqualification.

NO SYSTEMS ENGINEERING TOOLCHAIN KNOWLEDGE: The JD mandates DOORS or CodeBeamer for requirements management and Catia Magic for modeling. The candidate's toolchain is entirely Salesforce-native (Copado, Flosum, Jenkins, Jira) — there is no bridge between her skills and the role's technical requirements.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period stated in the resume; no penalty applied.
JD requires in-office presence in Chennai; candidate is based in Bengaluru.
Candidate has 7 years total experience, which exceeds half of the JD's 4-year relevant experience requirement.
JD gender preference is 'Any'; no penalty applied.
No college exclusion or preference specified in the JD; no adjustment applied.

availability_reason: Current role at Infosys is listed as August 2025 to Present with no end date or notice period mentioned.
alternative_role_fit: Senior Salesforce DevOps Engineer / Salesforce Release Manager
rare_assets: Selected as a Copado Mentor — a peer-recognised, invitation-only program indicating platform-level expertise recognised by the vendor itself.
Dual Flosum certifications at Professional and Expert level — rare combination as most practitioners hold only one.
self_reported_unverified: 'Rise Insta Award' for exceptional performance — award criteria and scope not described; cannot verify significance.
Claim of leading Salesforce DevOps for Philip Morris International — team size, scope, and ownership level are unverified.
interview_flags: Do not interview — candidate is in the wrong domain entirely; no radar, automotive, or embedded systems experience exists to probe.
If reconsidered for a Salesforce role, probe actual team leadership scope at Infosys — 'Technology Lead' title with no team size or direct reports mentioned.
Clarify the nature of the Apple project listed as 'August 2025 to Present' — this is a future date relative to most evaluation contexts; verify timeline accuracy.
Ask whether candidate has any academic or personal exposure to embedded systems, C/C++, or automotive protocols — to rule out any hidden crossover skills.
Verify Copado Mentorship program scope — was this a formal vendor-run program or an informal community initiative?
